text: During the initial exploration, the surgeon observes the sigmoid colon adhered to the lateral abdominal wall, containing multiple diverticula. Blood is visible in the pelvis, and an almost complete rectal lesion is identified. The two stumps of the lesion appear well supplied and clean, with regular margins.
Following an abundant pelvic washout, the surgeon places a pelvic drain and completes the procedure with the creation of a diverting loop ileostomy.
